mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is an AI Gateway that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It provides features such as cost tracking, load balancing, and logging, making it suitable for applications that require integration with multiple LLM services. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for proper resource allocation to handle multiple API calls efficiently.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/v1.30.0/bin/linux/amd64/kubectl

chmod +x ./kubectl

sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl

kubectl version --client

```

> Download kubectl binary for Linux.
> Make the kubectl binary executable.
> Move kubectl to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation of kubectl.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster on your preferred cloud provider with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30.0

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.
commands: gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> Fetch credentials for the cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add litellm https://berriai.github.io/litellm

helm install litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch6 --namespace litellm --create-namespace

```

> Add the LiteLLM Helm repository.
> Install LiteLLM in the litellm namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 --namespace litellm

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/monitoring/monitoring.yaml --namespace litellm

```

> Set up autoscaling for LiteLLM.
> Deploy monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment from your cluster. This will delete all associated resources, and any data stored in the application will be lost.

```bash

helm uninstall litellm --namespace litellm

kubectl delete namespace litellm

```

> Uninstall the LiteLLM Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace litellm > litellm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade litellm litellm/litellm --version 1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch6 --namespace litellm

kubectl get pods --namespace litellm

```

> Backup current LiteLLM resources.
> Upgrade LiteLLM to the latest version.
> Verify that the LiteLLM pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the container image could not be pulled, possibly due to incorrect image name or tag.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in your Helm chart and ensure it matches the available images.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is crashing repeatedly, possibly due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace litellm' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the LiteLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your nodes or adjust the resource requests in your deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs, improved logging features, and enhanced load balancing capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace litellm' showing all pods in the Running state.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.30.0
gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
